title: Pack a Carry-On Bag
summary: Pack a compliant carry-on bag that fits size limits and contains travel essentials.
learnerPromise: You will be able to pack a carry-on bag that meets airline size rules and holds everything you need for a flight.
whyItMatters: A well-packed carry-on saves you from checked bag fees and lost luggage worries. It also keeps your important items close during the flight so you are prepared and comfortable.
realLifeUses: Packing for a short trip without needing to check a bag
Keeping medications, chargers, and snacks in your carry-on for easy access
Making sure your bag fits in the overhead bin or under the seat
Preparing for a flight where you want to avoid baggage claim waits
materialsNeeded: Your travel items and a packing list
result: A fully packed carry-on bag that fits size limits and contains organized travel essentials.
youWillLearn: How to check your airline's carry-on size and weight limits
How to choose travel essentials and leave non-essentials behind
How to pack liquids and electronics to meet security rules
How to organize your bag for easy access during the flight
miniChallenge: Pack a carry-on bag for a two-day trip. Include clothing, toiletries, and one electronic device. Check that all liquids are in travel-sized containers and that the bag closes comfortably.
steps: Check your airline's carry-on size limits and measure your bag if needed.
Make a short packing list of essentials: clothing, toiletries, documents, chargers, and snacks.
Place liquids in a clear quart-sized bag and keep electronics easy to reach for security.
Roll or fold clothing to save space and place heavier items at the bottom of the bag.
Close the bag and lift it comfortably to confirm it is not too heavy to carry.
completionCriteria: Your bag fits within your airline's carry-on size limits
You packed travel-sized liquids in a clear bag and kept electronics accessible
The bag closes and is comfortable to lift and carry
commonProblems: Overpacking makes the bag too heavy or hard to fit in the overhead bin
Forgetting that liquids must be in small containers can cause delays at security
Important items get buried under clothing and are hard to reach during the flight
tips: Wear your bulkiest clothing on the plane to save space in your bag
Keep your ID, boarding pass, phone, and charger in an outer pocket for easy access
Pack a small empty water bottle and fill it after you pass through security
